title: Basic Science
description: Question batch for the Astronomy domain.

questions.question: What is the star at the center of our solar system?
questions.explanation: The Sun is our solar system's star.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red because of iron-rich dust.

questions.question: Which planet is the largest in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet.

questions.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.

questions.question: What is Earth's natural satellite?
questions.explanation: The Moon orbits Earth.

questions.question: What is a galaxy?
answers: A huge system of stars, gas, and dust
questions.explanation: Galaxies contain vast numbers of stars and matter.

questions.question: What is the name of our galaxy?
questions.explanation: Our solar system is in the Milky Way.

questions.question: What is a planet?
answers: A large body orbiting a star
A star making its own light
questions.explanation: Planets orbit stars and do not produce their own light.

questions.question: What is an asteroid?
answers: A small rocky body orbiting the Sun
questions.explanation: Asteroids are small rocky solar system bodies.

questions.question: What is a comet known for?
answers: A glowing tail near the Sun
questions.explanation: Comets can form tails when heated by the Sun.

questions.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
questions.explanation: Earth rotates on its axis, causing day and night.

questions.question: What causes Earth's seasons mainly?
questions.explanation: Earth's tilt changes sunlight angle through the year.

questions.question: What is a light-year?
answers: Distance light travels in a year
Time for Earth to orbit the Sun
questions.explanation: A light-year measures distance, not time.

questions.question: Which planet is famous for its large rings?
questions.explanation: Saturn is known for its bright ring system.

questions.question: What is a black hole?
answers: A region where gravity is extremely strong
An empty area with no matter
questions.explanation: Black holes have gravity so strong that light cannot escape.

questions.question: What is a nebula?
answers: A cloud of gas and dust in space
questions.explanation: Nebulae are gas and dust clouds in space.

questions.question: What do stars mainly produce by fusion?
questions.explanation: Fusion in stars releases huge amounts of energy.

questions.question: Which planet is known for supporting life as we know it?
questions.explanation: Earth is the only known planet with life.

questions.question: What is an eclipse?
answers: One body blocks light from another
questions.explanation: Eclipses happen when one body blocks another's light.

questions.question: What is the main source of the Sun's energy?
questions.explanation: The Sun shines through nuclear fusion.

questions.question: Why do we always see the same side of the Moon?
questions.explanation: The Moon is tidally locked to Earth.

questions.question: Why do stars seem dimmer than the Sun?
questions.explanation: Distance makes stars appear faint from Earth.

questions.question: What does a telescope mainly help with?
questions.explanation: Telescopes gather light and improve observation.

questions.question: Why does the Moon have phases?
answers: We see different sunlit portions as it orbits Earth
questions.explanation: Moon phases come from changing viewing angles.

questions.question: What is the asteroid belt?
answers: A region of many rocky bodies between Mars and Jupiter
A path of comets near Earth
questions.explanation: The asteroid belt lies mainly between Mars and Jupiter.

questions.question: Why is Jupiter important in the solar system?
answers: Its huge gravity strongly influences other bodies
It is closest to the Sun
It has liquid water oceans on the surface
questions.explanation: Jupiter's mass gives it major gravitational influence.

questions.question: What is a dwarf planet?
answers: A round body orbiting the Sun that has not cleared its orbit
questions.explanation: Dwarf planets orbit the Sun but do not dominate their orbital zone.

questions.question: Why do astronomers use radio telescopes?
answers: To detect radio waves from space
questions.explanation: Radio telescopes observe radio emissions from space.

questions.question: What best explains the Big Bang model?
answers: The universe expanded from a very hot dense state
The Sun exploded to form planets
questions.explanation: The Big Bang model describes an expanding universe from a hot dense beginning.

questions.question: Why do astronomers say looking far away is looking back in time?
questions.explanation: We see distant objects as they were when their light began traveling.
